description: Chen Su Lan Methodist Children’s Home (CSLMCH) is dedicated to transforming the lives of children and families in need. Our mission is to foster healing, growth, and self-reliance within our community, emphasizing family reunification and improved psycho-social well-being. About the role A qualified executive with experience in teaching and delivering individualized intervention to join our team of multidisciplinary professionals who embraces the best interest of child and youth. He/she is committed towards the methodical planning, coordinating and executing of all educational-related policies/programmes/activities. The primary role of the executive is to provide tiered intervention to children/youths in consultation with the multidisciplinary team to promote the child/youth’s academic and personal excellence. The Education Executive is also responsible for working closely with key stakeholders to plan, organize and implement programmes and activities for the children and youths, manage volunteers and conduct needs and outcome assessments to measure and enhance programme relevance and effectiveness to bring about quality service and standards. Key Responsibilities: A) Delivers effective and efficient individualised intervention to children/youths Manage children and youths’ behaviour for the purpose of providing a safe and positive learning environment. Create a positive learning culture using a trauma-informed inclusive education approach in the House of Wisdom. Utilize data-based decision-making and evidence-based strategies to provide Individual Educational Plans (IEPs) and intervention plans for the children/youths with their respective case executives and in consultation with the Supervisor. Working with the school teachers to identify learning objectives for lesson planning with differentiated instruction. Plan, prepare, and deliver small group intervention in Social Emotional Learning to develop learners’ social-emotional and life skills. Provide academic support for learners according to their abilities and in alignment with mainstream or special school curriculum objectives. Coordinate and collaborate with colleagues e.g., caseworkers, therapy team and external stakeholders e.g. parent/guardians, schools, tutors, and volunteer tutors to deliver effective interventions to increase academic and personal success. Establish positive, safe, and trusting relationships with children/youths, their families and school teachers to encourage academic and personal progress specific to their needs. Commit to the continual professional development of not less than 50 training hours in any 2 years, which shall consist of at least 10 hours in Pedagogy training to keep current with the latest skills and ensure professional competency, subject to approval by the immediate supervisor. B) Plan, organize and execute programmes and activities relevant and beneficial for the children and youths . To communicate and work with potential stakeholders such as organisations, vendors and volunteers, to bring in appropriate educational programmes that meet the needs of children/youths. Track the attendance of participants of the programme and plan for the tuition scheduling of the volunteers/tutors on a monthly basis. Coordinate and/or support centre’s planned events and programmes. Develop and facilitate programmes that need support. Conduct periodic needs and outcome assessments to evaluate the effectiveness and relevance of key programmes. C) Regularly assesses children’s/youths progress to modify instruction/intervention Make use of relevant data to identify child’s/youth’s needs, modify instruction, and report progress to all stakeholders. Summarize assessment results and incorporates information into an appropriate support plan. Prepare relevant submissions/reports on a termly or ad-hoc basis regarding children/youths’ learning, progress development and recommendations for intervention. Maintain updated and accurate records which adhere to high levels of confidentiality. Coordinate, facilitate, and maintain records on meetings with parents, school teachers, and internal/external stakeholders. D) Provides consultation to volunteer tutors, school teachers, parents, and other stakeholders to foster greater academic and social/emotional skill development across all children and youths Manage volunteers; conduct virtual and on-site training sessions for volunteers. Offers ongoing support or training to volunteer tutor, colleagues, teachers, and parents in order to accelerate child/youth’s growth. Participate in a multi-disciplinary team of professionals by utilizing a problem-solving approach. - Prepare and disseminate professional resources according to child’s/youth’s needs. Establish and maintain ongoing professional collaboration with internal and external stakeholders in order to maximize children/youths’ impact in all areas of development. E) Development of teaching curriculum and resources Ongoing development and review of curriculum and pedagogy with instructional materials to ensure the education programme at House of Wisdom remains dynamic, evidence-based, and responsive to the learners’ varying needs. F) Provide Academic Pathways Guidance & Career Guidance Provide education and career counselling to children/youths based on their learning abilities and career/academic aspirations so as to make good academic choices/career prospects. Coordinate, partner with/network with various Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) or companies for visits, learning journeys or taster workshops. G) Supported Employment - Develop individualized employment plans with youth and incorporate input from the caseworkers when appropriate. Adjust the plan according to the youth’s needs and preferences. Conduct job development and job search activities. Assess the client’s vocational functioning on an ongoing basis. Provide individualized on-site job coaching to assist youths. Provide education and support to employers, which may include negotiating job accommodations and on-site client supervision and support with frequent employer communication. Prioritize employer relations and solutions-based thinking through timely and proactive communication. Use conflict resolution skills to provide timely interventions. Return phone calls, follow up on job leads, and meet with youths consistently to resolve job issues. H) Any other duties Review and update all educational policies, SOPs, programmes, children/youths’ education case file/data/information. Perform the secretariat role for planned and/or ad-hoc meetings where required. Perform administrative tasks related to volunteer programmes and funding applications for programme reporting and audit purposes. Take on any other assignment, project or duty as assigned by the Supervisor. Candidate's Requirements: At least 2 years of experience working within special education, providing learning support or community rehabilitation services for adults or youths at risk. Relevant qualifications in education, special education or psychology. Demonstrate flexibility, strong work habits, and a positive attitude, with a commitment to teamwork and collaboration. Knowledge and experience in profiling and delivering interventions for individuals with social-emotional difficulties are preferred. Experience in administrative tasks, including for fund application for programmes. Attend meetings and willing to take minutes when required. Working Hours Monday to Friday during 9am to 6 pm or 12:30pm to 9:30pm The selected candidate is required to work in the evenings three times a week as needed. What are you waiting for: Apply now and join us today with a dedicated team committed to making a difference in the lives of vulnerable children and families. You’ll have the opportunity to lead impactful initiatives, collaborate with passionate colleagues, and contribute to meaningful community-driven solutions. We work closely with community partners to bring holistic support and care to the children and families. Together we make a difference on the lives of the children in the Home. Together We Nurture Every Child and Family to Shine. Only Shortlisted applicant will be notified.

As part of a therapy hub, our therapists work with a wide range of client profiles and are deployed across different services based on organisational needs. You will be supporting children with diverse speech, language, communication, and feeding needs in THK’s range of paediatric-focused programmes, including early intervention programmes (e.g., EIPIC), Development Support and Learning Support (DS-LS), Children Therapy Spaces, and other initiatives. Therapists may be deployed across various paediatric programmes depending on service requirements. Develop and implement Individualised Intervention Plans (IIPs) or Individualised Educational Plans (IEPs), working collaboratively with families and other professionals. Deliver therapy through individual and/or group sessions in centre-based or community-based settings. Professional Development Opportunities Access to clinical supervision, mentorship, and ongoing professional development. Opportunities to work alongside experienced therapists and multidisciplinary teams supporting children across a broad range of developmental needs. Support planning and implementation of activities beyond routine programme (e.g. outings). Review escalation of abnormal changes in clients’ conditions, when necessary. Communicate new programmes, interventions, and/or changes to a client’s health / circumstances to the CCAs or the Clinical Team. Quality assurance Gather data to support infection control and prevention reporting. Support reporting officer in preparing and gathering documents for internal and external Audits. Supervise and support execution of new non-clinical standard operating procedures (SOPs) by Care staff. Volunteer management Supervise volunteer activities. Provide requirements plan for volunteers. Gather feedback on volunteers and share with relevant teams (e.g. volunteer management department). Plan and coordinate collaboration for volunteer activities. Professional development Participate in relevant training programmes to acquire skills and knowledge that enhances work performance. Leadership and team management Gather information on staff learning requirements and conduct learning needs analysis with care staff. Manage training schedules and coordinate with training and education department. Conduct total performance appraisal for subordinates. Mentor subordinates to develop their competencies at work and work towards progression in their career. Others Plan budget for operations and monitor the expenditure. Ensure the preventive and corrective maintenance of equipment and infrastructure are performed timely and effectively. Perform other duties as and when required or instructed by reporting officer or Head of Department. REQUIREMENTS Diploma or degree in healthcare related industry or other relevant field of study. Working in a geriatric set up, long term care or nursing home set up will be an advantage but not necessary. At least 2 years supervisory experience. Experience in operations or managing a business unit is preferred. Experience as leader in process improvement projects and/or innovation and technology projects would be an advantage. Basic proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel and Power point, and other computer skills. Able to multi task and work in a stressful environment. Good interpersonal and communication skills Caring, compassionate and able to communicate and show empathy towards clients. title: Play Therapist / Senior (Counselling Centre)
topic_label: occupational therapy, special education, language therapy
description: Senior / Play Therapist (Counselling Centre) JOB DESCRIPTION FOR SENIOR / PLAY THERAPIST The Senior Play Therapist will work closely with children, parents, families, and other professionals to assess and treat emotional, behavioral, and developmental issues in children using play therapy and other therapeutic approaches. This role includes providing direct therapy, conducting assessments, developing treatment plans, and offering guidance and supervision to junior therapists. Additionally, the Senior Play Therapist will mentor and supervise the team of play therapists, contribute to program development, and lead community outreach initiatives. Roles & Responsibilities: Clinical Practice: Provide individual and group play therapy to children with a variety of emotional, psychological, and developmental challenges (e.g., trauma, anxiety, depression, behavioural disorders, and grief). Use a range of play therapy techniques (e.g., non-directive play therapy, directive play therapy, art therapy, sand tray therapy) to help children express emotions and resolve psychological difficulties. Conduct systematic needs assessment and service planning including intake evaluation, treatment assessment, planning and evaluation. Conduct detailed psychological assessments, including intake evaluations, therapeutic assessments, and treatment planning. Develop, implement, and monitor individualized treatment plans based on each child’s unique needs and progress. Integrate play therapy techniques with other therapeutic modalities, such as Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT), Dialectical Behavioural Therapy (DBT), and psycho dynamic therapy, etc., when necessary. Provide crisis intervention and ongoing therapeutic support when needed. To attend to individual clients and other centre needs when there is no Play Therapy intervention work. Therapeutic Supervision & Leadership: Supervise and mentor junior play therapists and other clinical staff, providing clinical guidance, feedback, and professional development. Lead clinical case discussions and offer expert consultation to the team on complex cases. Take on a leadership role in fostering a collaborative team environment focused on holistic, client-centred care. Develop and facilitate internal training workshops on play therapy techniques, child development, and therapeutic interventions for team members. Family & Caregiver Support: Work closely with parents, caregivers, and family members to ensure they are actively involved in the therapeutic process. Provide psychoeducation to parents regarding child development, therapeutic approaches, and how to support their child’s emotional well-being at home. Conduct family therapy when appropriate to strengthen family dynamics and improve communication and emotional connections. Administrative Responsibilities: Maintain accurate and timely documentation for all therapy sessions, progress notes, assessments, and treatment plans in compliance with professional and organizational standards. Ensure that all practices comply with the relevant laws, ethical guidelines, and regulations, including those set by the Singapore Association for Counselling (SAC) and the Ministry of Health (MOH). Participate in regular team meetings, clinical audits, and quality assurance initiatives to ensure the delivery of high-quality therapeutic services. Community Outreach & Advocacy: Engage in community outreach activities to raise awareness about the benefits of play therapy and child mental health services. Represent the organization in external meetings, conferences, and public forums related to child therapy and mental health advocacy. Collaborate with external professionals such as schools, paediatricians, social workers, and other healthcare providers to ensure comprehensive and integrated care for children. Support centre-based initiatives, outreach and services. Key Requirements: Education & Qualifications: Master’s degree in Counselling, or a related field. Certification in Play Therapy (e.g., Registered Play Therapist or equivalent) is required. Additional training or certification in psychotherapy, child development, or family therapy is highly desirable. Registration with the Singapore Association for Counselling (SAC), Association for Play Therapy (APTS), or equivalent body is preferred. Experience: Minimum of 2 years of clinical experience in play therapy, with a demonstrated track record of working with children, adolescents, and families or atleast 5 years experience with Supervision skills and experience for a Senior role. Experience in psychotherapeutic interventions for children with emotional and behavioural challenges, such as anxiety, depression, trauma, and grief. Experience in developing individualized treatment plans. Proven ability to supervise and mentor junior therapists in a clinical setting. Therapeutic Competencies: Expertise in a variety of play therapy approaches (preferably), including non-directive play therapy, directive play therapy, and sand tray therapy. Strong understanding of child development and the ability to adapt therapeutic interventions based on a child’s developmental stage. Competence in integrating psychotherapy techniques such as Cognitive Behavioural Therapy (CBT) and Dialectical Behavioural Therapy (DBT) with play therapy approaches (preferably). Strong diagnostic skills, with the ability to assess and treat a wide range of emotional and behavioural challenges in children. Leadership & Supervision: Strong leadership abilities with experience in supervising and mentoring junior therapists. Ability to collaborate effectively with multidisciplinary teams to ensure holistic and client-centred care. Strong communication skills for both interpersonal interactions and written documentation. Thank you for your interest and your application. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified for interview.

title: Freelance Private Psychologist/Counsellor ( 1-To-1)
topic_label: occupational therapy, special education, language therapy
description: Job Description: Freelance Private Psychologist/Counsellor (1-To-1) APPLY HERE: https://portal.specialminds.com.sg/?type=service_professional Company Overview: We are Singapore’s first dedicated matching platform for children with learning or special needs. Our mission is to connect children with freelance professionals across five key areas: Tuition, Early Education, Early Intervention, Therapy & Counseling, and Enrichment Activities. We strive to create a supportive community that bridges students' needs with expert guidance, helping them grow and thrive academically and emotionally. Position: Freelance Private Psychologist/Counsellor (1-To-1) Responsibilities: Conduct comprehensive assessments to evaluate children’s emotional, behavioural, and psychological needs. Develop and implement individualised treatment plans based on assessment findings and each child’s unique needs and goals. Provide one-on-one counselling or therapy sessions, utilising evidence-based techniques to address issues such as anxiety, depression, behavioural challenges, and social skills deficits. Collaborate with families, caregivers, and other professionals to provide holistic support and ensure consistency in therapeutic interventions across environments. Maintain accurate and confidential documentation of each child’s progress, adjusting treatment plans as necessary to achieve optimal outcomes. Provide guidance and resources to families, helping them understand and support their child’s mental health needs. Stay informed about current trends, research, and best practices in child psychology and counselling. Requirements: A recognised degree in Psychology, Counseling, or a related field. Valid registration with the Allied Health Professions Council (AHPC) or relevant professional body in Singapore. Previous experience working with children, particularly in a therapeutic or counselling context, is highly valued. Strong knowledge of child development, mental health issues, and therapeutic techniques. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a compassionate and empathetic approach to working with children and their families. Ability to work independently and customise therapy plans based on each child’s progress and evolving needs. A genuine passion for supporting children’s mental health and emotional well-being. Why Join Us? Enjoy Higher Rates : Our rates are adjusted for inflation and aligned with the latest market standards, ensuring you are fairly compensated. No More Sudden Cancellations : We collect deposits before the first session, so you don’t have to worry about last-minute cancellations. No Wasted Trips : If a client doesn’t show up, you will still receive compensation for the first session. One-Time Administration Fee : Any additional sessions or direct referrals won’t incur further administrative fees from Special Minds. Boost Your Profile : Gain visibility through the free promotion of your profile on our social media platforms, helping you connect with more clients. Meaningful Career : With the growing demand for special needs education (SEN) in Singapore, this is your opportunity to gain valuable experience and build a meaningful career helping students with unique needs. Additional Income Opportunity : Take on assignments that fit your schedule and preferences to earn extra income in your free time, or choose full-time opportunities for more consistent earnings! Compensation: Part-Time Tutor : Up to $60/hr Full-Time Tutor : Up to $90/hr Early Childhood Educator : Up to $70/hr MOE/Special Ed Tutor : Up to $140/hr Enrichment Specialist (dance, art, swimming, etc.): Up to $120-150 per session Therapist (speech, occupational, physiotherapy, etc.): Up to $200-220/hr Psychologist : Up to $250/hr Counsellor : Up to $150/hr If you’re passionate about education and want to make a difference in the lives of students with learning or special needs, join us at Special Minds. Together, we can create a brighter future for every child. APPLY HERE: https://portal.specialminds.com.sg/?type=service_professional For more information, please visit us at: https://specialminds.com.sg/ If you have further queries, please email us at hello@specialminds.com.sg , or contact us at 8721 8722.

title: Groceries Packer (North) [EL]
topic_label: senior food technologist, food technologist, food manufacturing
description: The Groceries Packer is responsible for accurately and efficiently packing rice and egg products for delivery or sale. This role ensures products are handled with care, meet quality standards, and are packaged according to company guidelines. Maintaining cleanliness and following food safety protocols are critical aspects of the role. Key Responsibilities Packing Operations Sort and pack rice and egg products into designated packaging materials (e.g., bags, trays, boxes). Weigh and measure rice to ensure accurate quantities as per product specifications. Inspect eggs and rice for quality before packing, discarding any damaged or substandard items. Label packages with necessary details, including product information, batch numbers, and expiration dates. Inventory Management Keep track of packing materials and notify the supervisor of low stock. Ensure all packed products are correctly logged in the inventory system. Quality Assurance Follow company standards for product quality and appearance. Maintain consistent checks to ensure rice and eggs meet packaging requirements. Hygiene & Safety Compliance Adhere to all food safety and hygiene regulations, including proper handling and storage of products. Maintain cleanliness and organization in the packing area. Wear personal protective equipment (PPE) as required. Team Collaboration Work closely with the production and logistics teams to ensure timely order fulfillment. Assist in resolving packing issues or bottlenecks in the workflow. Other Duties Perform additional tasks as assigned by the supervisor to support operational needs. Qualifications & Requirements High school diploma or equivalent preferred. Prior experience in food packaging or warehouse operations is an advantage. Strong attention to detail and ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment. Physical ability to stand for long periods and lift packages up to [Insert Weight, e.g., 20-25 kg]. Familiarity with food safety standards (e.g., HACCP, GMP) is a plus. We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. title: Storeman
topic_label: senior food technologist, food technologist, food manufacturing
description: About the Role We are looking for a skilled and reliable Storeman to join our kitchen team. The storeman job responsibilities are: Inventory & Stock Control Receive and check incoming goods against delivery orders and invoices. Verify quantity, quality, expiry dates, and packaging condition. Record stock movement accurately in inventory system or stock cards. Maintain FIFO / FEFO practices for all food items. Conduct regular stock counts and assist in monthly stock take. Any other work duties assigned by superior. Storage & Handling Storw raw materials, ingredients, and packaging correctly in dry, chiller, or freezer areas. Ensure proper labelling, segregation, and cleanliness of storage areas. Monitor temperature and storage conditions according to food safety requirements. Maintain good housekeeping and hygiene standards at all times. Issuance & Coordination Issue ingredients and supplies to kitchen production based on requisitions. Coordinate with kitchen, procurement, and QA teams on stock availability. Flag low stock levels, discrepancies, damages, or expired items promptly. Food Safety & Compliance Comply with SFA / HACCP / ISO / internal food safety standards. Follow pest control, hygiene, and sanitation procedures. Support audits, inspections, and traceability documentation. General Duties Assist with unloading, loading, and internal stock transfers. Operate basic warehouse equipment (pallet jacks, trolleys, etc.). Perform other ad-hoc duties as assigned by supervisor.
